t_ch: 不过，稍微有点特殊的是，我专打与假面愚者和幻造种有关的官司，在这个光怪陆离的世界里替人摆平所有规则性烦恼。
t_en: Though I do have a bit of a specialty, I handle cases involving Masked Fools and imagenae. In this wild, bizarre world, I sort out all kinds of rule-related headaches for people.

t_ch: 假面愚者？
t_en: Masked Fools?

t_ch: 是啊，想像一下，你是个老实巴交的上班族，清清白白，从没听过什么「世界尽头」酒馆，也对假面愚者是否存在不置可否，但是——
t_en: Yeah, just picture that you're an honest, hardworking office worker. Squeaky clean record. Never even heard of the "World's End" Tavern, couldn't care less whether the Masked Fools are real or not, but...

t_ch: 忽然有一天，你在街边撞上了某个大叔，还对他嘴臭了两句。接下来每一天都不对劲了：麦片罐子里能倒出蟑螂，水管子里淌出泔水…你终于明白，自己得罪了某个愚者。
t_en: One day, you bump into some random dude on the street and run your mouth at him. From that day on, everything goes sideways: cockroaches pour out of your cereal box, sludge flows from the tap... You finally realize you've ticked off a Fool.

t_ch: 假面愚者这么讨人厌啊？
t_en: Are Masked Fools really that annoying?

t_ch: 也不全是，哈哈。
t_en: Well, not all of them, haha.

t_ch: 假面愚者这么小心眼？
t_en: The Masked Fools are that petty?

t_ch: 大部分…是这样的。能力越大，心眼越小，恶作剧越可怕。
t_en: Most of the time... yeah. The more powerful they are, the pettier they get, and the worse their pranks become.

t_ch: 总之，普通人烦他们就像讨厌家里的蟑螂，或者落在西装上的鸽子粪…假面愚者的恶作剧算是某种不可抗力吧。
t_en: Anyway, normal folks find them as annoying as cockroaches in the house, or pigeon droppings on a suit... I guess the Masked Fools' pranks are just one of those unavoidable things in life.

t_ch: 我就专替大家解决这样的麻烦和误会，让所有人和蟑螂、鸽子粪还有不可抗力冰释前嫌，得到相应的精神损失、误工费和医药费补偿。
t_en: I specialize in resolving these kinds of troubles and misunderstandings, helping everyone bury the hatchet with cockroaches, pigeon droppings, and inevitable forces of nature, and getting the compensation they deserve for emotional distress, lost wages, and medical expenses.

t_ch: 幻造种？
t_en: Imagenae?

t_ch: 是啊，想像一下，你是某个亿万富翁的私生子…失礼了！算你是婚生子吧。
t_en: Yeah, imagine this: you're some billionaire's illegitimate kid... Sorry, my bad! Let's say you're legitimate.

t_ch: 你巴巴地盼着你的爸爸早日咽气，好继承他那不知多少的遗产。你满心欢喜数着日子盼望骄奢淫逸的日子早点到来。终于——
t_en: You're eagerly waiting for your old man to kick the bucket so you can inherit his untold fortune. You're counting down the days with glee, dreaming of living it up in luxury. Finally...

t_ch: 终于，你爸永生了？
t_en: So finally, your old man achieved immortality?

t_ch: 不，你爸嗝屁了。
t_en: Nope, your dad kicked the bucket.

t_ch: 终于，你爸嗝屁了？
t_en: So your old man finally kicked the bucket?

t_ch: 不，你爸永生了。
t_en: Nah, your old man's immortal now.

t_ch: 这么说吧，你爸的肉体完蛋了，但基于人死前强烈的执念，一个「你爸的幽灵」应运而生，并且宣称自己对他的财产拥有全部支配权。
t_en: Put it this way: your dad's body kicked the bucket, but thanks to his intense obsession right before death, a "ghost of your dad" came into being, and it's claiming full control over all his assets.

t_ch: 这个时候，你最佳的选择，就是找我。在你爸的幽灵到底算不算你爸的问题上，我也许能帮你争取到一些发言权…和财产。
t_en: At a time like this, your best bet is to come to me. When it comes to the whole "is your dad's ghost really your dad" question, I might be able to help you secure some say in the matter... and some inheritance.

t_ch: 听了那么多，你有什么官司想打吗？
t_en: So, after hearing all that, got any cases you wanna take to court?

t_ch: 没有。
t_en: No.

t_ch: 真没有。
t_en: I really don't.

t_ch: 我明白，我明白。大部分法律服务的对象正是你这样还不了解世界运转规则的小年轻。放心吧，只要还在二相乐园，你迟早会有用上我的时候。
t_en: I get it, I get it. Most of my legal services are for young folks like you who don't quite grasp how the world works yet. Don't worry, as long as you're in Planarcadia, sooner or later you'll need my services.

t_ch: 收好这张名片，回见。
t_en: Keep this card. Catch you later.

t_ch: 又见面了，{F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}，需要法律援助吗？
t_en: Well, we meet again, {F#young lady}{M#young man}. Need any legal assistance?

t_ch: 有时间聊聊吗？
t_en: Do you have time for a chat?

t_ch: 当然没问题，你想了解什么？
t_en: Sure thing, what would you like to know?

t_ch: 本次咨询按秒计费，但您的一部分影子可折抵首小时费用。
t_en: This consultation is billed by the second, but a portion of your shadow can be used to offset the first hour's fee.

t_ch: 你到底是什么存在啊？
t_en: What the heck are you, anyway?

t_ch: 呃…律师？
t_en: Uh... A lawyer?

t_ch: 别走，我不是在故意浪费你的时间。毕竟我丢失了脑袋——对于过去，我一概不知。但打从有记忆起，我似乎都奔波在幻造种和假面愚者的法律官司里。
t_en: Wait, don't go. I'm not trying to waste your time here. After all, I lost my head, so I literally know nothing about my past. But ever since I can remember, I've been caught up in legal battles between imagenae and the Masked Fools.

t_ch: 所以，我前身可能是个丢了脑袋的假面愚者…吧。那么，伞会是我的面具吗？
t_en: So... my past self might've been a Masked Fool who lost their head, I guess? Then would the umbrella be my Mask?

t_ch: 介绍一下你的服务？
t_en: What kind of services do you offer?

t_ch: 我专打与假面愚者和幻造种有关的官司，在这个光怪陆离的世界里替人摆平所有规则性烦恼。
t_en: I specialize in handling cases involving Masked Fools and imagenae. In this wild, bizarre world, I sort out all kinds of rule-related headaches for people.

t_en: Well, not all of them, haha.

t_en: The Masked Fools are that petty?

t_en: Most of the time... yeah. The more powerful they are, the pettier they get, and the worse their pranks become.

t_en: Nope, your dad kicked the bucket.

t_ch: 不，你爸永生了。
t_en: Nah, your old man's immortal now.

t_en: At a time like this, your best bet is to come to me. When it comes to the whole "is your dad's ghost really your dad" question, I might be able to help you secure some say in the matter... and some inheritance.

t_ch: 案件胜率如何啊？
t_en: How's your win rate on cases?

t_ch: 作为一名经验丰富的律师，我通常会将自己的社会阅历与专业能力相结合，为不同客户专门定制最适合的服务方案。
t_en: As an experienced lawyer, I typically combine my social insights with professional expertise to create the most suitable service plan for each client.

t_ch: 比如面对身材矮小的噗噜，我会派遣人高马大的欧克助理们和对方热情谈判，从而达到感化对象，让对方知难撤诉的效果。
t_en: For example, when dealing with a vertically challenged ploo, I'd send my towering orch assistants to have a "friendly negotiation" with them, which usually convinces them to see the light and drop their lawsuit.

t_ch: 至于打扰到雇主生活和工作的假面愚者，那就好办多了。大家都在「酒馆」出没，抬头不见低头见…如果他不想自己也遭遇同等份量的恶作剧，多半愿意考虑赔偿的。
t_en: As for any Masked Fools who disturb an employer's life and work, that's even easier to handle. Everyone hangs out at the "tavern," everyone runs into each other all the time... If they don't want to get hit with the same level of pranks themselves, they'll probably be willing to consider compensation.

t_ch: 瞧，解决问题的方法有很多，法律只是其中一种手段。
t_en: Look, there are plenty of ways to solve a problem. The law's just one of 'em.

t_ch: 先聊到这吧。
t_en: That's enough chit-chat for now.

t_ch: 希望下次见面时，你能带着案子委托来找我…我是说，有需要的话。
t_en: I hope the next time we meet, you'll come to me with an assignment... I mean, if you ever need my help.

t_ch: 没事，就来打个招呼。
t_en: Nothing much, just dropping by to say hi.

t_ch: 哈哈，我也很高兴见到你。如果你之后遇到了什么需要法律援助的情况，随时联系我。
t_en: Haha, I'm glad to see you. If you ever need legal assistance in the future, feel free to contact me anytime.

t_ch: 祝你生日快乐！祝你生日快乐！祝你生日快哎哎哎乐！祝你生日快乐！
t_en: Happy birthday to you! Happy birthday to you! Happy birthday to youuuu! Happy birthday to you!

t_ch: 祝你前程似锦，运气爆棚，走哪儿哪儿亮堂堂。花开富贵人长乐，日进斗金福满堂。顺风顺水，万事称心。好运常相伴，霉运不打搅。
t_en: May your future be bright as silk, your luck be through the roof, and your path be lit with glory. May your fortune bloom and joy be endless, with gold pouring in day after day. May the winds be at your back and every wish come true. Let good luck walk beside you always, and pray bad luck never finds your door.

t_ch: 你干什么呢？
t_en: What are you doing?

t_ch: 这不是显而易见吗？说吉祥话，给我被关在储物柜里的兄弟输送愿力。
t_en: Isn't it obvious? I'm sending some Wishpower to my buddy who's trapped in the locker by speaking words of good fortune.

t_ch: 这哪里显而易见了？你倒是好好给人家解释啊！
t_en: How is that obvious? You'd better explain it properly!

t_ch: 多谢多谢，你也是。
t_en: Thank you, thank you. Same to you!

t_ch: 我正靠说吉祥话，输送愿力给我被关在储物柜里的兄弟呢！你别抢我兄弟的吉祥话啊！
t_en: I'm sending Wishpower to my buddy in the locker by speaking words of blessing! Don't steal the words of luck!

t_ch: 谁会抢你的吉祥话啊？「抢」和「吉祥话」是可以组合到一起的词语搭配吗？
t_en: Who's gonna steal your words of luck? And since when did "steal" and "words of luck" belong in the same sentence?

t_ch: 罗瓦，我的好兄弟！我祝你福如太平洋长流水！寿比奥林匹斯山不老松！
t_en: Lova, my dear buddy! May your fortune flow endlessly like the waves of the Pacific Ocean! And may you live as long as the immortal pines of Mount Olympus!

t_ch: 你这吉祥话到底是哪个异世界的啊？哪有太平洋啊？咱们这儿哪来的太平洋啊？你刚哭出来的啊？
t_en: What kind of alternate universe did that blessing come from? "The Pacific Ocean"? What "Pacific Ocean"? Did you just cry yourself a new ocean or something?

t_ch: 算我求你了，你再努力回忆一下储物柜的密码吧！别给我说吉祥话了！
t_en: I'm begging you, try harder to remember the locker code! Stop giving me that "good luck" nonsense!

t_ch: 你们这有奥林匹斯山？
t_en: You guys have Mount Olympus here?

t_ch: 这是重点吗！重点是不要再说吉祥话输送愿力了，快放我出来啊！
t_en: That's not the point! The point is to stop talking about channeling Wishpower with words of fortune. Just get me out of here already!

t_ch: 吉祥话真能输送愿力吗？
t_en: Can you really transmit Wishpower using lucky words?

t_ch: 理论上可以，但幻造种能不能以此维生…我不想用自己做这个实验啊！
t_en: It's theoretically possible, but whether imagenae can survive on that... I've no wish to be the test subject for this experiment!

t_ch: 唉，我也不指望能打开这扇门了，只希望能多说两句吉祥话，让我兄弟在储物柜里过得好受一些。
t_en: *Sigh* I've given up on opening this door. I just hope saying a few words of blessing can make my buddy's time in the locker a bit more bearable.

t_ch: 你倒是指望一下啊！被关起来的是我诶！你不要擅自替我放弃啊！
t_en: Have some faith, will you! I'm the one locked up in here! Don't you give up on me!

t_ch: 你要是有空，也可以陪我们兄弟俩聊聊天。我兄弟一个人住在储物柜里太没意思了。
t_en: If you have some time, you can come hang out and chat with me and my buddy. It's no fun for him to live alone in that locker.

t_ch: 那不叫「住在」好吗？
t_en: You can't really call this "living," can you?

t_ch: 这次密码肯定对！啊…还是不对…稍等兄弟，我先给你来段生日歌补充一下愿力！祝你生日快乐！祝你生…诶？{NICKNAME}，你来陪我们聊天了吗？
t_en: This password's gotta be the one! Ah... no, that's not it either... Hold on buddy, let me sing you a birthday song to boost my Wishpower! Happy birthday to you! Happy birth— Huh? {NICKNAME}, are you here to chat with us?

t_ch: 那就陪你们聊聊吧。
t_en: Alright, we can chat for a bit.

t_ch: 太好了。你看，兄弟，即使被困在储物柜中，也还是会认识新的朋友。这就证明了一件事——这个储物柜不隔音。
t_en: Great! See, buddy, you can still make new friends even when trapped in a locker. Which proves one thing: this locker isn't soundproof.

t_ch: …这用你证明啊？
t_en: ...Was that something that needed to be proven?

t_ch: 所以到底发生了什么？
t_en: So what exactly happened here?

t_ch: 唉…事情是这样的，我们之前想要做一个恶作剧。就是让我兄弟躲在储物柜里，然后骗我们其他朋友打开柜子，他就可以蹦出来吓人一跳。
t_en: *Sigh*... Well, this was supposed to be a prank, and the plan was to have my buddy hide in the locker, then trick our other friends into opening it so he could jump out and give them a good scare.

t_ch: 这是我这辈子最后悔的决定。
t_en: This has got to be the worst mistake I've made in my life.

t_ch: 没想到最后我忘掉了密码，我兄弟就这样被关在里面了。
t_en: Who would've thought that I'd forget the password and leave my buddy locked in there?

t_ch: 我计算过了，储物柜的密码有8位数，如果我每天8小时不停尝试，每5秒穷举一个密码，那么只需要差不多<unbreak>50</unbreak>年就可以找到密码了！
t_en: I did the math, the locker has an 8-digit code. If I spend 8 hours a day trying combinations non-stop and testing one code every 5 seconds, I'll crack it in about <unbreak>50</unbreak> years!

t_ch: 你这数学算的对吗？我才不要在储物柜里关<unbreak>50</unbreak>年啊！你当是封印什么魔兽呢？
t_en: Are you sure your math is right? I'm not gonna be locked in a locker for <unbreak>50</unbreak> years! What do you think I am, some sealed mythical beast?

t_ch: 但也有好消息！我听说泊地站的站务人员工作非常懈怠，他们完全懒得定期清理储物柜。
t_en: But there's good news! I heard that the staff at Perdido Station are super lazy. They can't even be bothered to clean out the lockers regularly.

t_ch: 好在哪儿呢我请问？
t_en: And what's so good about that, may I ask?

t_ch: 好就好在，我可以慢慢尝试密码，不必担心他们中途打扰。这样总有一天，我会找到正确的密码的！
t_en: The good thing is, I can try out the passwords at my own pace without worrying about them interrupting me. One of these days, I'll definitely crack the code!

t_ch: …要不你还是走吧，留我在这儿自生自灭吧。
t_en: ...Why don't you just leave me here to fend for myself.

t_ch: 真能靠吉祥话补充愿力吗？
t_en: Can you really replenish Wishpower with words of fortune?

t_ch: 「愿力」的本质就是「愿」嘛！那「美好的祝愿」肯定也是愿力的一种咯。所以吉祥话、生日歌、祝福语里面当然也是富含愿力的。
t_en: The essence of "Wishpower" is "wishes" after all! So "good wishes" must be a form of Wishpower too. That's why auspicious phrases, birthday songs, and blessings are naturally filled with Wishpower.

t_ch: 我只要每天努力对着储物柜唱生日歌、说吉祥话，我兄弟就能接收到充足的愿力，不至于崩解成尘灵。
t_en: As long as I sing happy birthday and speak auspicious words to the locker every day, my buddy here will receive enough Wishpower to keep from disintegrating into a wispae.

t_ch: 你看我给你演示一下啊！罗瓦！我祝愿你福如尼罗河长流水！寿比乞力马扎罗山不老松！
t_en: Let me show you what I mean! Lova! May your fortune flow like the Nile River! And may your life be as eternal as the ancient pines of Mount Kilimanjaro!

t_ch: 兄弟，算我求你了，能不能不要再若无其事地往吉祥话里掺杂异世界地名了？跟个伪人似的，真的很吓人……
t_en: Hey buddy, I'm begging you, could you please stop casually dropping the names of otherworldly places into your well-wishes? It's seriously creepy how you do it like it's nothing, as if you're some kind of impostor...

t_ch: 没有其他办法可以开门吗？
t_en: Is there any other way to open the door?

t_ch: 实在没办法啊！我也想让站务员帮忙开门，但是自己钻到储物柜里，这是违反车站运营条例的，会被站务员罚款的！我兄弟不富裕，不能让他赔钱。
t_en: There's no other way! I wanted to ask the station staff to help open the door, but crawling into the storage locker is against station regulations. They'd fine us! My buddy isn't exactly rolling in cash, so I can't let him take that hit.

t_ch: 这种时候就不要替我心疼钱了好吗？
t_en: Can you not worry about my money at a time like this?

t_ch: 兄弟，你不能这么说。别人不心疼你，我心疼你。别人不给你讲吉祥话，我给你讲吉祥话。
t_en: Hey, you can't talk like that. Even if no one else cares about you, I do. If there is no one out there who will give you words of blessing, I will.

t_ch: …你还是别心疼我了。要不你找个工具把柜子撬开吧？
t_en: ...Don't worry about me. Why don't you find a tool to pry this locker open instead?

t_ch: 兄弟，不可以。破坏公物是违反道德的。被关在储物柜中，黑暗的只是你的身体；但违反了你心中的道德律，黑暗的可是你的心灵啊！
t_en: You can't do that. Vandalism is morally wrong. Being locked in a locker only traps your body in darkness, but violating the moral code in your heart? That darkens your very soul!

t_ch: 都这种时候了你给我灌什么心灵鸡汤啊！
t_en: Cut the inspirational talk, this is not the time!

t_ch: 只摄取生日歌和吉祥话容易营养不良，我这不是给你换换口味吗？
t_en: Consuming only birthday songs and well-wishes can lead to malnutrition, I'm just giving you a change of flavor, aren't I?

t_ch: 好兄弟，真的，我不开玩笑——我一出来就弄死你。
t_en: Listen up, buddy. I'm being dead serious here, but the moment I get out, you're toast.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你刚才有幸见证了我们兄弟二人结下生死之誓的时刻！是不是很感动？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you just witnessed the epic moment where my buddy and I swore an oath of life and death! Pretty dramatic, huh?

t_ch: …造孽啊。
t_en: ...What a mess.

t_ch: 先聊到这里吧，我还有事。
t_en: Let's wrap up here, I've got stuff to do.

t_ch: 好的，下次再见～有空再来找我们聊天哦！记得给我们写信，收件地址写这个储物柜就好！
t_en: Alright, see you next time~ Drop by for a chat when you're free! Don't forget to write to us! Just address your letters to this locker!

t_ch: 没有这种收件地址！不要给邮递员添麻烦！
t_en: That address doesn't exist! Don't make the mailman's job harder!

t_ch: 先聊到这里吧，我还有事。
t_en: Let's wrap up here, I've got stuff to do.

t_ch: 好的，下次再见～有空再来找我们聊天哦！记得给我们写信，收件地址写这个储物柜就好！
t_en: Alright, see you next time~ Drop by for a chat when you're free! Don't forget to write to us! Just address your letters to this locker!

t_ch: 没有这种收件地址！不要给邮递员添麻烦！
t_en: That address doesn't exist! Don't make the mailman's job harder!

t_ch: 乐子神在上，今年的律师资格证考试迫在眉睫，请保佑我进试顺利成为律师！
t_en: Oh, The Laughter above, the bar exam is just around the corner... Please bless me to pass and become a lawyer!

t_ch: 呼——啊啊，我还是不敢看签文！
t_en: *sigh* Aaah, I still can't bring myself to read the fortune slip!

t_ch: 我来帮你看吧。
t_en: Let me look at it for you.

t_ch: 你…你来看？好像确实可以！那就拜托你了！我有很强的预感，今年的律师资格证考试一定是上上签，毕竟我都考五年啦！
t_en: Y—You'd do that? That would be perfect! I'm counting on you! I've got a really strong feeling that I'll ace the bar exam this year... I mean, it's my fifth attempt, after all!

t_ch: 他颤抖着将一张签文交给你。你打开一看，上面冷冰冰写着：「下下签」。
t_en: With trembling hands, he passes you a fortune slip. You open it and read the cold, stark message: "Woe."

t_ch: 结果怎么样？
t_en: How are the results?

t_ch: 是上上签！
t_en: It's "Great Fortune!"

t_ch: 是、是吗！果然，我就说感觉自己今天神清气爽！
t_en: R—Really!? I knew it, I was feeling particularly refreshed today!

t_ch: 是中签。
t_en: It's mid.

t_ch: 中签也好！你知道吗？中签才是最好的签，因为意味着自己还有上升空间！
t_en: Okay, fine. I'll take midling luck. You know what? It is actually the best fortune, because it means there's still room for improvement!

t_ch: …是下下签。
t_en: ...It's "Great Woe."

t_ch: …你要不再看一次？有可能是打开方式不对！
t_en: ...Maybe you should check it again? You might have opened it wrongly!

t_ch: 哈哈，你怎么不笑？没关系啦，欢愉一点我的朋友，大不了再考一年！乐子神一定是觉得我前几天有些懈怠，在提醒我分秒必争！
t_en: Haha, why aren't you laughing? Cheer up, my friend, worst case I'll just retake it next year! The Laughter must've noticed I've been slacking off lately and is reminding me that every second counts!

t_ch: 这种东西没什么好信的。
t_en: You can't trust stuff like that.

t_ch: 你将签文揉成一团，丢了。哇哦，真酷！
t_en: You crumpled up the fortune slip and tossed it away. Wow, that's so cool!

t_ch: 没错！如果把这些祈愿的时间全用来备考，我都能刷完两套题了！
t_en: You got that right! If I'd spent all this wishing time studying, I could've knocked out two practice sets by now!

t_ch: 反正无论如何，我进试一定要成功成为律师！
t_en: No matter what, I must succeed in becoming a lawyer!

t_ch: 毕竟——我，可是一岁抓周就抓住「历年律师资格证考试真题」，励志要成为律师的男人啊！
t_en: After all, I'm the man who grabbed Past Bar Exam Papers during my first birthday divination ceremony, destined to become a lawyer!

t_ch: 为了这个理想，我在那窄小的房间里，不吃不喝不睡连续复习了十四天，成功攻克了<unbreak>927</unbreak>条小论文难点！
t_en: For this dream, I locked myself in that tiny room, went without food, water, or sleep for fourteen straight days, and successfully conquered <unbreak>927</unbreak> thesis problems!

t_ch: 你看，这就是律师资格证考试的魅力，我都突破人类极限了！
t_en: See? That's the charm of bar exams: I've transcended human limits!

t_ch: 那是人的极限，但不是幽灵的。
t_en: That's the limits for humans, but not for ghosts.

t_ch: 朋友，难怪你好像有点死了！
t_en: No wonder you look kinda dead, my friend!

t_ch: 别开玩笑了，虽然第四天我确实心脏抽了一下，但你看我的身体，这不还……
t_en: Come on, don't joke around! Sure, my heart skipped a beat on day four, but look at me now, I'm still...

t_ch: <size=28>——我怎么变透明了！</size>
t_en: <size=28>Why am I turning transparent!</size>

t_ch: 那十四天我满眼都是题，根本没有注意到这些啊！
t_en: For those fourteen days, all I could see were exam questions! I didn't notice any of this stuff!

t_ch: 不过，如果真是幽灵的话……
t_en: But if I've really turned into a ghost...

t_ch: 幻造种律师资格证考试年龄限制是很宽松的！这样一来我就不用担心超过年龄限制，可以一直考律师资格证了！我真是好死！
t_en: The age limit for the imagenae bar exam is super flexible! This means I don't have to worry about aging out! I can keep taking the bar exam forever! I'm so dead lucky!

t_ch: 感谢《灵物保护法》，幽灵也可以报考律师资格证考试…放心吧，就算成为幽灵，我进试也会成功当上律师！
t_en: Thanks to Faekind Protection Act, even ghosts can take the bar exam... Don't worry, even as a ghost, I will still become a lawyer!

t_ch: 想了解一下你的人生…
t_en: I'd like to know more about your life...

t_ch: 当然可以，就当做是模拟面试了吧～但你不要用那种同情的眼神看着我！
t_en: Sure thing, let's treat it as a mock interview~ But don't give me that look of pity!

t_ch: 为什么想当律师？
t_en: Why do you want to be a lawyer?

t_ch: 乐子神在上，律师可是二相乐园人人艳羡的神仙工作啊！
t_en: By The Laughter, being a lawyer is the most envied celestial job in all of Planarcadia!

t_ch: 其实五年前我刚刚毕业时，也曾勇闯公司大厂，试图做一个左手咖啡右手笔记本的优雅办公族。直到我参加了公司的群面。
t_en: Actually, when I first graduated five years ago, I boldly ventured into a big corporate life at IPC, trying to be one of those elegant office workers with coffee in one hand and a laptop in the other. That was until I had to do the group interview.

t_ch: 五个应届毕业生对着「1+1=2」的小学数学题绞尽脑汁。有的说要从原理开始证明，有的猜这是公司新的研究方向，还有个噗噜现场做了七十页幻灯片汇报！
t_en: Five fresh graduates were racking their brains over an elementary math problem "1+1=2." One wanted to prove it from first principles, another thought it was IPC's new research direction, and there was even a ploo who made a <unbreak>70</unbreak>-page PowerPoint presentation about it!

t_ch: 答案不就是「正确」吗？非要弄这么复杂，非要显出能耐，这就是浪费资源的恶性内卷，是社会的积弊和毒瘤！
t_en: Isn't the answer just "correct"? Making things needlessly complicated, trying to show off... That's just a toxic rat race, a deep-rooted cancer in our society!

t_ch: 但律师就不会了，工资高、受人尊敬、还能匡扶正义，这才是人类欢愉的尽头。
t_en: But a lawyer? Now that's different. High salary, respect from others, and you get to uphold justice. Now that's the peak of human elation.

t_ch: 所以我放弃了公司的录取机会，发誓要成为律师！
t_en: So I gave up my job offer from the IPC and swore to become a lawyer!

t_ch: 想过做别的事情吗？
t_en: Ever thought about doing something else?

t_ch: 老实讲，放弃公司的机会后，我还真没想过参加律师资格证考试之外的路子。
t_en: To be honest, after giving up on IPC, I never really thought about any path other than taking the bar exam.

t_ch: 都说现在平台大机会多，我这样一路优秀，从施耐德大学附属中学一路考到施耐德大学经济学院的优秀毕业生不愁出路，可事实上我会干、能干的事还真不多。
t_en: Everyone says there are plenty of opportunities out there these days. But even as someone who's been excellent all along from Schnider University's affiliated middle school, all the way to graduating with honors from Schnider University's Political Economics Department, there really aren't many things I'm actually good at or capable of doing.

t_ch: 人情世故我烦得很，喝酒待客我又做不来…思来想去，我这样的应届毕业生果然还是最擅长且精通考试！
t_en: I can't stand all that social etiquette stuff, and I'm terrible at drinking and entertaining... After thinking it through, a fresh graduate like me is definitely best at and most skilled in taking exams!

t_ch: 我能刷题，我爱背书，我擅长把考试的时间规划到毫厘。我只要备考就高兴，就觉得自己可有价值，可有未来！
t_en: I can grind practice problems, I love memorizing textbooks, and I'm great at planning my exam time down to the millisecond. Just studying for exams makes me happy. It makes me feel valuable, like I have a future!

t_ch: 而且大家不都说考律师资格证好吗？放着阳光大道不走，那些小径谁知道会把我带到哪条阴沟里去。所以我还是只想律师资格证考试。
t_en: Besides, doesn't everyone say getting a law license is the way to go? Why take some sketchy side path when there's a bright, sunny road ahead? Who knows which gutter those other paths might lead to. So yeah, I'm sticking with the bar exam.

t_ch: 欸，朋友，你是做什么工作的？
t_en: Hey friend, what do you do for a living?

t_ch: 我是大明星。
t_en: I'm a superstar.

t_ch: 我是到处旅游的。
t_en: I travel all over the place.

t_ch: 我到处行善积德。
t_en: I'm spreading good karma everywhere I go.

t_ch: 那听起来就是「自由职业」？太不稳定了。
t_en: Sounds like "freelancing" to me? That's way too unstable.

t_ch: 听我一句劝：「常在水里游，总会撞噗噜。」还是多刷真题，尽早找个稳定工作吧！
t_en: Take my advice: "If you swim in the water too much, you'll eventually run into a ploo." Better grind through more practice questions and find a stable job sooner!

t_ch: 关于变成幽灵…
t_en: About becoming a ghost...

t_ch: 哎，当你沉迷刷题，进入心流状态的时候，你也会忘记自己的身体吧……
t_en: Ah, when you're deep into grinding practice problems and get into that flow state, you tend to forget about your own body too...

t_ch: 不过现在再回想起那一天，确实也有些端倪。我只感觉心脏有些不适，但它不是不跳，反倒越跳越快，因为我刷出了人生最高分的卷子！
t_en: But thinking back to that day now, there were some signs. My heart felt a bit off, but it wasn't that it stopped beating... Quite the opposite, it was racing because I'd just scored the highest marks ever on my test!

t_ch: 然后我眼前黑了一下，就一下，我就立刻强撑着又把眼睛睁开了。
t_en: My vision went dark for a moment, just a split second, but I immediately forced my eyes back open.

t_ch: 因为刚好到平时给我妈打电话的点了嘛，我得跟她汇报好消息！
t_en: It's about time for my usual call with mom. I've got to tell her the good news!

t_ch: …对了，我妈…还不知道呢……
t_en: ...Oh right, my mom... she doesn't know yet...

t_ch: 还是先别告诉她了。等我当上了律师，也许我死掉的消息也会变得更好接受一些。
t_en: Better not tell her just yet. Maybe the news of my death will be easier to accept after I become a lawyer.

t_ch: 现在复习得怎么样？
t_en: How's your preparation going?

t_ch: 五年没考上律师，我真没脸再赖在家，所以租了个小房专门备考。
t_en: After five years of failing to the bar exam, I was too ashamed to keep living at home, so I rented a small place just to focus on studying for the exam.

t_ch: 我整天埋头在四平米的小屋，也不出门，也不跟朋友玩，就是埋头做题，虽然痛苦如坐牢，但好在效果已经很显著了！
t_en: I've been cooped up in my tiny <unbreak>40</unbreak>-square-foot room all day, not going out, not hanging with friends, just grinding through practice tests. It feels like prison, but hey, at least it's paying off big time!

t_ch: 现在，我已经能把「二相乐园律师资格证考试人类卷历年真题」倒背如流了！
t_en: Now, I can recite the "Planarcadia Bar Exam: Human Edition Past Papers" backwards and forwards!

t_ch: …等等…「人类卷」？
t_en: Wait... Human Edition?

t_ch: 如果我没记错…幽灵…考的是…「幻造种卷」……
t_en: If I remember correctly... ghosts... should be taking the... Imagenae Edition...

t_ch: <size=28>这下全完啦！！！！</size>
t_en: <size=28>I AM TOTALLY SCREWED!!!!</size>

t_ch: 但没关系！既然我已经死了，那换句话说，时间就是站在我这边的！心若在梦就在！大不了从头再来！
t_en: But that's okay! Since I'm already dead, in other words, time is on my side! Where there's a will, there's a way! Worst case scenario, I'll start over from scratch!

t_ch: 加油，下次聊。
t_en: Good luck, catch you later!

t_ch: 好啊我的朋友，我一定不辜负你的鼓励，会努力成为律师的！要是遇到什么难题，记得找我帮你辩护！
t_en: You got it, my friend. I won't let your encouragement go to waste. I'll work hard to become a lawyer! If you ever run into any legal conundrums, remember to hit me up for defense!

t_ch: 加油，下次聊。
t_en: Good luck, catch you later!

t_ch: 好啊我的朋友，我一定不辜负你的鼓励，会努力成为律师的！要是遇到什么难题，记得找我帮你辩护！
t_en: You got it, my friend. I won't let your encouragement go to waste. I'll work hard to become a lawyer! If you ever run into any legal conundrums, remember to hit me up for defense!

t_ch: 初次见面~客人！欢迎来到砰砰萌萌咖啡店，我是这里的机铠服务员，MD<unbreak>-78</unbreak>-8，昵称「八子」❤
t_en: Nice to meet you~ dear guest! Welcome to Lovey Dovey Café, I'm MD<unbreak>-78</unbreak>-8, the mechatron server here, but you can call me Octyx ❤

t_ch: 请问客人需要些什么服务？
t_en: What services can I provide for you?

t_ch: 这边提供什么？
t_en: What do you have here?

t_ch: 嗯嗯，客人这样问的话——当然是什么都行❤喔！我是说饮品啦～
t_en: Hmm~ Since you asked... Anything goes, of course ❤! I mean, drinks-wise~

t_ch: 我走错了？这里真是咖啡店吗？
t_en: Did I take a wrong turn? Is this really a coffee shop?

t_ch: 是咖啡店呢~咦…是八子❤让您误会了吗？
t_en: It most definitely is a café~ Oh... pardon, is it what I ❤ said that confused you?

t_ch: 为每一位到店的客人提供最周到的服务和最美味的咖啡——这就是砰砰萌萌咖啡店的愿景。
t_en: To provide everyone who visits our shop with the most attentive service and the most delicious coffee, that's the vision of Lovey Dovey Café.

t_ch: 竟然一共有五十多种口味任君品尝呢！包含咕嘟咕嘟燃油饮、网红混搭缤纷苏乐达…其中的爱心萌萌❤魔法杯是这条街的必喝榜第一名喔！
t_en: There are over <unbreak>50</unbreak> flavors for you to try! Including Glug-Glug O-Fuel, the viral Fusion Rainbow SoulGlad... and Sweetlove ❤ Magic Cup is the number one must-drink on this street!

t_ch: 这条街就一家咖啡店吧…
t_en: But there's only one coffee shop on this street, isn't it?

t_ch: 虽然是这样没错啦，但是我们店也有自己的特色哦！
t_en: Well, that's true and all, but our shop has its own special charm!

t_ch: 不是苏打豆汁儿我不喝。
t_en: I won't drink anything but mung bean soda.

t_ch: 只要客人需要，所有的饮品都可以满足~
t_en: Any drink you desire, we can make it for you~

t_ch: 作为服务员，我也会提供各类服务。比如，在为客人上餐后，八子会为客人施展扑通扑通心跳魔法，让您的每一口咖啡都幸福满满。
t_en: As the server, I offer all kinds of services. For example, after serving your meal, I will cast a thump-thump heart-throbbing spell, making every sip of your coffee filled with pure bliss.

t_ch: 又见面了，客人！欢迎光临砰砰萌萌咖啡店，机铠服务员八子一直在等你哦❤
t_en: We meet again! Welcome to Lovey Dovey Café, your mechatron waitress, Octyx, has been expecting you ❤

t_ch: 客人还有什么问题吗？
t_en: Do you have any other questions?

t_ch: 的确有些问题…
t_en: Yeah, I do have some questions...

t_ch: 不论什么疑问，我都会尽量为客人解答的！
t_en: Whatever questions you have, I'll do my best to answer them!

t_ch: 你们这儿有什么定制服务？
t_en: What custom services do you offer here?

t_ch: 八子的定制服务包括：情绪校准、幸福回路激活、重工业ASMR、心事缓存、运算噪声占卜、未来波动幸运测算……
t_en: Octyx's custom services include: emotional calibrations, happiness circuitry activation, heavy industrial ASMR, cognitive load buffering, divination predictions, future fluctuations and assorted fortunes...

t_ch: 嗯…还有顾客会用几系统时仔细观察我身上的零件，八子也不知道这有什么好观察的……
t_en: Hmm... Some customers would spend a few system hours carefully examining the parts on my body. I really don't get what's so interesting about that...

t_ch: 不过，只要客人喜欢，那就没问题哦！
t_en: But as long as you like it, then it's all good!

t_ch: 平日生意怎么样？
t_en: How's business these days?

t_ch: 当然是分旺季和淡季的啦……
t_en: Well, it depends on whether it's peak season or off-season...

t_ch: 最近幻月游戏举办期间，八子就超~级忙！收集的愿力也比平时多多了，如果真的化为实体金币，我走路的时候一定会叮当响的！
t_en: During the recent Phantasmoon Games, I've been suuuper~ busy! I collected way more Wishpower than usual. If it were actual gold coins, I'd be jingling with every step!

t_ch: 不过，大部分时候八子的愿力是不够维持生存的…对于我们机铠来说，如果这样下去，会慢慢变成二维的设计图的……
t_en: But most of the time, my Wishpower isn't enough to sustain life... For us mechatrons, if this continues, we'll slowly turn into 2D blueprints...

t_ch: 但八子想出了一个非常好的办法，一旦愿力不够，八子就会主动变成一堆零件，躺在咖啡店前的地上。
t_en: But I came up with a brilliant solution: Whenever I run low on Wishpower, I'd voluntarily break down into a pile of parts, lying on the ground in front of the coffee shop.

t_ch: 没有过路的机铠爱好者会拒绝从零到一的拼装机会！在他们为八子组装身体的时候，就已经为我提供了足够的愿力啦！
t_en: No mechatron enthusiast passing by would turn down a chance to build from scratch! While they were assembling my body, they already provided me with enough Wishpower!

t_ch: 而且，这部分客人还会顺道为咖啡店提供收入，真是一举两得呢~
t_en: Plus, these customers will bring in extra income for the coffee shop. Talk about killing two birds with one stone!

t_ch: 嗯…能够服务更多的客人，也很让八子开心呢！
t_en: Hmm... being able to serve more customers also makes me really happy!

t_ch: 你一直这样元气满满吗？
t_en: Are you always this full of energy?

t_ch: 当然——为客人提供最周到的服务，是让八子最快乐的事情。
t_en: Of course! Providing the most attentive service to you is what makes me the happiest.

t_ch: 只是八子偶尔也有些失落呢…毕竟人类真的好难懂。
t_en: But sometimes I feel a bit down... after all, humans are really hard to understand.

t_ch: 八子每天勤勤恳恳地做到不出错，却没能让顾客们开心起来；反而偶尔搞砸了，把咖啡弄到身上，总会引来怜爱的目光……
t_en: Despite working diligently every day without making mistakes, I still couldn't make every customer happy. I even messed up and spilled coffee on myself, which would always attract sympathetic glances...

t_ch: 后来我才从客人们那里知道：偶尔犯错不是缺点，反而是一种萌点呢！
t_en: Later, I learned from the customers that making the occasional mistake isn't necessarily a flaw, but actually kind of endearing!

t_ch: 人类，真是难懂呀！
t_en: Humans are so hard to figure out!

t_ch: 还是下次再来光顾吧。
t_en: I should come back here another time.

t_ch: 再见，客人，八子的爱意魔法始终伴随着你！
t_en: Goodbye, friend! My love magic will always be with you!

t_ch: 还是下次再来光顾吧。
t_en: I should come back here another time.

t_ch: 再见，客人，八子的爱意魔法始终伴随着你！
t_en: Goodbye, friend! My love magic will always be with you!

t_ch: 唯一让八子有些沮丧的是…好像这样做招来的客人比八子正常工作还多……
t_en: The only thing that's got me feeling down is... somehow this scheme's bringing in more customers than when I was actually working...

t_ch: 欢迎光临无限<unbreak>11</unbreak>便利店，请随意挑选。
t_en: Welcome to Infinity <unbreak>11</unbreak> Convenience Store. Feel free to look around.

t_en: Leave.

t_en: Welcome to Arcadia "RECORDS," where we keep all our favorite music. Feel free to listen to your heart's content. If you need any help, just give me a shout!

t_ch: 随便看看。
t_en: Look around.

t_en: Leave.

t_en: Welcome to "BOOK-ON!" comic store! The latest chapter of Fluffy Across the Blue just arrived, and it's our top recommendation. Of course, we also sell various other books. If you can't find what you're looking for, feel free to give me a shout!

t_ch: 随便看看。
t_en: Look around.

t_ch: 哇！恭喜你成为今天第<unbreak>100000</unbreak>个呼唤「回归吧！火火火花！」的幸运观众~♥
t_en: Wow! Congratulations on being today's <unbreak>100,000</unbreak>th lucky viewer to call out "Come back, Spar-Spar-Sparxie!"~♥

t_ch: 你将获得「和火花的人生四十秒」互动时间！请说出你的心愿吧~握手，To签，合影，聊天——也许还会触发令人惊喜的隐藏奖励噢！
t_en: You'll get forty seconds of interactive time with Sparxie! Tell us your wish~ Do you want a handshake, an autograph, a photo, a chat. You might even trigger some surprising hidden rewards!

t_ch: 你是本尊吗？
t_en: Is that really you?

t_ch: 是呀，我是你的火花花呀~♥
t_en: That's right, I'm your Sparxicle~ ♥

t_ch: 拜托，能如此近距离和火花花互动的机会可不多呀~
t_en: C'mon, it's not every day you get the chance to interact with Sparxicle up close and personal like this~

t_ch: 我找花火。
t_en: I'm looking for Sparkle.

t_ch: 拜托，能如此近距离和火花花互动的机会可不多呀~
t_en: C'mon, it's not every day you get the chance to interact with Sparxicle up close and personal like this~

t_ch: 简直不亚于能有幸亲眼一睹狗熊骑独轮车、狮子钻火圈、猴子顶碗了…你确定要将时间浪费在辨认一个旧型号、旧面具上么…小、灰、毛~
t_en: It's like being lucky enough to witness a bear riding a Unicycler, a lion jumping through fire hoops, or a monkey balancing bowls... Are you sure you want to waste your time identifying an old model, an old mask... Gray... Hair~

t_ch: 还是说…你找到了新的乐子，是来邀我参演的！我愿意！
t_en: Unless... you've found a fresh source of amusement and want me to co-star! Oh, I'm in. I am so in!

t_ch: 不如现在就来一场即兴的选角面试吧！快，向我提问~♥
t_en: How about we do an impromptu casting audition right now! Come on, hit me with your questions~ ♥

t_ch: 恭喜你获得「和火花的人生四十秒」互动时间！
t_en: Congratulations! You've won forty seconds of interactive time with Sparxie!

t_ch: 请说出你的心愿~握手，To签，合影，聊天…嘻嘻，火花花的美好品德之一，就是有求必应，有问必答噢~♥
t_en: Tell me your wish~ A handshake, an autograph, a photo together, a chat... Hehe, one of Sparxicle's best qualities is being always here to help and answer any question~ ♥

t_ch: 那我们聊个四十秒的吧。
t_en: Then let's chat for forty seconds.

t_ch: 你对我的好奇真的只值四十秒么？别骗人啦~
t_en: Is your curiosity about me really only worth forty seconds? C'mon, don't kid yourself~

t_ch: 火花是怎么诞生的？
t_en: How was Sparxie born?

t_ch: 动动手指，注册账号；动动脑筋，选定赛道；再动动嘴巴——哈喽哈喽，欢迎欢迎，感谢感谢——当然，这还只是一张没有灵魂的面具~
t_en: A flick of the finger, and you've registered. A bit of brainwork, and you've picked your niche. Then, a quick wag of the tongue, "Hi everyone, welcome to the channel, don't forget to like and subscribe." Of course, that's just a mask with no soul behind it.

t_ch: 成为风靡二相乐园的主播「火花」，还需要把握住时代的流量密码~很简单，你就直接问——观众老爷们想听什么，想看什么呀～
t_en: To become the hottest streamer "Sparxie" that takes Planarcadia by storm, you've gotta crack the code to viral success~ It's easy. Just ask directly: What do the honored audience want to hear or see~?

t_ch: 想看表演，那就又唱又跳~马上他们就会张大嘴巴，手指蠢蠢欲动，心想——原来身体可以这么灵活！我也想试试！
t_en: If it's a show they want, give 'em the full song and dance! Before you know it, they'll be staring wide-eyed, itching to join in, thinking: "How can someone move like that? I've gotta try it for myself!"

t_ch: 想听故事，那就和他们聊天~然后他们会瞪大眼睛——原来外面世界的人们活得那么精彩呀！我也想看看！
t_en: If it's a story they're after, then just strike up a conversation! Before long, their eyes will be wide with wonder: "So the world outside is really that vibrant? I want to go see it too!"

t_ch: 世界真真假假，公司高高在上，劳苦疲倦时，人们会发现乐子最是纯粹～在「火花」这里，他们可以是被服务的主人公，甚至成为主人公！
t_en: The world is a blur of truth and lies, and the IPC sits high above it all. When the grind becomes too much to bear, people realize that "Elation" is the only thing that's pure. With Sparxie, they don't just get to be the star of the show, they can actually become the protagonist!

t_ch: 于是…人见人爱的「火花」就这样诞生在人们对欢愉的渴求里~♥
t_en: And so... the universally beloved "Sparxie" was born from humanity's craving for the Elation~ ♥

t_ch: 只是可惜，水能载舟亦能覆舟呀。
t_en: Too bad, the very thing that lifts you up can also bring you down.

t_ch: 黑红也是红。
t_en: Any publicity is good publicity.

t_ch: 黑红？什么！火花花真的被大家讨厌了么，呜呜，这真是…太好了！当所有人都以为你在破防时，这场戏才真正进入高潮！
t_en: Black and red? What! Does everyone really hate Sparxicle now? Boo hoo, this is just... perfect! When everyone thinks you're losing it, that's when the show truly reaches its climax!

t_ch: 你就不好奇我的下一场戏什么时候开场么～放心，不会让你等太久的，毕竟…观众的耐心可是很有限的～
t_en: Aren't you curious when my next act begins~? Don't worry, I won't keep you waiting long. After all... an audience's patience is quite limited~

t_ch: 准备什么时候复出？
t_en: When are you planning to make your comeback?

t_ch: 小灰毛，你在关心我的下一场戏什么时候开场，对吧？放心～不会让你等太久的，毕竟…观众的耐心可是很有限的～
t_en: Gray Hair, you're wondering when my next act's gonna start, right? Don't worry~ I won't keep you waiting long. After all... an audience's patience only goes so far~

t_ch: 主播日常都播些什么？
t_en: What does the streamer usually broadcast?

t_ch: 最开始嘛，就是跳跳舞，没有人会不喜欢看好看的人动起来吧？但主播最不喜欢的就是成为一张标签，将一件事反复翻炒啦——
t_en: At first, I just danced. I mean, who doesn't love watching attractive people move, right? But the last thing this streamer wants is to be pigeonholed and have people rehash the same old thing over and over—

t_ch: 深夜读评论的知心朋友，评价热点话题的毒舌，直播恐怖游戏的胆小鬼，潮到风湿的穿搭与美妆教学，ASMR资本论诵读哄睡服务……
t_en: Late-night comment-reading confidant, sharp-tongued critic of trending topics, scaredy-cat horror game streamer, drip-or-drown fashion and makeup tutorials, ASMR bedtime readings of socio-capital economics...

t_ch: 全能主播时代的领航员和守塔王，可不是开玩笑的哟~♥
t_en: The all-around streamer era's navigator and tower defense champion isn't just for show, you know~ ♥

t_ch: 我也想当主播，教教我。
t_en: I wanna be a streamer too. Teach me how!

t_ch: 首先，忘掉「真实的你」～再给自己捏一张恰到好处的假面，七分人设，两分悬念，剩下一分留给观众想象。然后，学会把一切当做素材——
t_en: First, forget about the "authentic you"~ Then craft yourself a perfectly-fitted mask. <unbreak>70%</unbreak> character design, <unbreak>20%</unbreak> mystery, and leave the last <unbreak>10%</unbreak> to your audience's imagination. After that, learn to treat everything as material!

t_ch: 你的眼泪是流量，你的愤怒是热点，你的哈哈大笑是噱头，而你此刻的求知欲…不就是最好的直播预告吗~
t_en: Your tears are clout, your anger is a hotspot, your laughter is a gimmick, and your curiosity right now... isn't that the perfect teaser for my next stream?

t_ch: 为什么不专精一个赛道？
t_en: Why not specialize in one niche?

t_ch: 「赛道」这种画地为牢的东西早过时啦！你瞧啊——在厨房跳舞，顺便煮碗拉面，舞蹈区和美食区的流量不就同时到手了？
t_en: This whole "staying in your niche" idea is so last month! Look! Dance in the kitchen while making noodles, and boom! You're raking in clout from both the dance community AND the foodies at the same time!

t_ch: 开一场「匿名忏悔室」直播，让观众用虚拟货币购买其他人的秘密，情感咨询和金融议题的关注度也轻易拿捏～
t_en: Start an "Anonymous Confessional" livestream where viewers can use virtual currency to buy other people's secrets. Easy way to rack up views on relationship advice and financial hot topics~

t_ch: 真正的狂欢，就是把所有赛道的栏杆全部拆掉！让万事万物都成为你的玩具~
t_en: A real party means tearing down all the guardrails on every niche! Let everything in the universe become your playground~

t_ch: 聊聊「幻月游戏」？
t_en: Wanna talk about the Phantasmoon Games?

t_ch: 规则上说：每一位谒者参与游戏后至少需要完成三个重要行动——
t_en: According to the rules, each Supplicant must complete at least three key actions after joining the game...

t_ch: 「唱名」——噢，就是面具为你背书的简历包装；「奇迹」——单位时间内的爆款指标；「宣扬」——就像是把一模一样的笑话复制粘贴到每个人的脑子里。
t_en: Roll-call: Oh, that's your resume getting jazzed up by your mask. Miracle: That's just the viral hits measured by the minute. Promotion: That's like copy-pasting the same joke into everyone's mind.

t_ch: 「获胜」是唯一的选项，为了觐见阿哈，所有人都攀炎附热，根本忘了最初的「欢愉」才不是满屏的弹幕与喝彩。规则，竞赛，输赢——可是欢愉最忌讳的玩意啦！
t_en: "Victory" is the only option, and in order to be blessed with an audience with Aha, everyone's bowing to the powerful and completely forgotten that the original Elation wasn't about screens full of bullet comments and cheering crowds. Rules, competition, winning and losing. Those are the things Elation hates most!

t_ch: 你要知道，最棒的表演永远不在剧本里~
t_en: Remember, the best performances are never in the script~

t_ch: 你见过阿哈吗？
t_en: Have you ever seen Aha?

t_ch: 你不想见阿哈吗？
t_en: Don't you wanna meet Aha?

t_ch: 你信不信，其实每个人都见过阿哈？
t_en: Believe it or not, everyone's actually met Aha before?

t_ch: 与其见祂，或者浪费一分钟成为祂，不如好好酝酿一个让所有人捧腹大笑的笑话！整一个大活！你怎么知道阿哈没躲在你的热门视频后看热闹呢？
t_en: Instead of trying to meet THEM, or wasting time trying to become THEM, why not spend that time cooking up a joke that'll have everyone doubled over in laughter? Let's pull off something legendary! After all, how do you know Aha isn't hiding behind your viral video right now, just watching the chaos unfold?

t_ch: 当你把生活直播成一场连阿哈也忍不住偷看的演出时，谁还需要那张觐见的门票呢~
t_en: When you livestream your life into a show so entertaining that even Aha can't help but peek, who needs a ticket to an audience anyway~

t_ch: 你还有多少张「面具」？
t_en: How many more "masks" do you have left?

t_ch: 小灰毛，不要掉入外行视角的陷阱了呀~你这个问题就像在问一位魔术师有多少口袋一样。
t_en: Gray Hair, don't fall into the trap of an outsider's POV~ That question is like asking a magician how many pockets they have.

t_ch: 你怎么不数数我们见了几次面呢？也许我们的每一次相遇都是不同的面具在与你对话噢？而你的每一句话也都为我下一张面具设计提供了参考！你人真好！
t_en: Why don't you count how many times we've met? Maybe each of our encounters was a different mask talking to you! And every word you say gives me ideas for my next mask design! You're such a big help!

t_ch: 所以偷偷告诉你~我还有一张「主角」面具！它承载了花火最强烈的勇气，最纯粹的开拓信念，和最难以抵挡的吸引力，她也可以为了拯救一个世界战斗到最后！
t_en: So I'll let you in on a little secret~ I've got one more mask: the "Protagonist"! It holds Sparkle's most intense courage, her purest conviction for the Trailblaze, and the most irresistible charisma. With it, she can fight to the very end to save an entire world!

t_ch: 小心点哟，也许未来某一天，我会抢走本属于你的戏份~
t_en: Watch yourself~ One of these days, I might just steal the spotlight right out from under you~

t_ch: 没什么要问的了
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 我还有好多秘密，保持你的好奇，我都会告诉你的，只要你敢信~♥
t_en: I've got loads more secrets to share. Just keep that curiosity of yours alive, and I'll tell you everything... if you dare to believe me~ ♥

t_ch: 下次再聊！
t_en: Talk later!

t_ch: 我还有好多秘密，保持你的好奇，我都会告诉你的，只要你敢信~♥
t_en: I've got loads more secrets to share. Just keep that curiosity of yours alive, and I'll tell you everything... if you dare to believe me~ ♥

t_ch: 主角大驾光临！你好呀，球棒浣熊。
t_en: The star of the show has arrived! Hey there, Baseball Raccoon.

t_ch: 刚恶补完一期绒绒号，都怪你们的故事太精彩了！
t_en: Just binge-watched an entire episode of The Fluffy. It's all your fault for having such amazing stories!

t_ch: 嗯——睡了那么久，谁懂一觉醒来就蹲到好作品的幸福感？
t_en: Mmm... After sleeping for so long, who can truly understand the bliss of waking up and immediately stumbling upon a masterpiece?

t_ch: 我懂！
t_en: I get you!

t_ch: 我们果然是同好嘛，击个掌！
t_en: I knew we were kindred spirits! High five!

t_ch: 还有更精彩的。
t_en: And that's not even the best part.

t_ch: 那我可要期待一下了。
t_en: Well, I'll be looking forward to it, then.

t_ch: 没看错，你就是……
t_en: No way, you're really...!

t_ch: 咳咳，再自我介绍一下——
t_en: Ahem, let me introduce myself again.

t_ch: 我叫绯英，幻月游戏特邀观察员，电子宠物专业饲养员，永远青春靓丽的高中生…唔，但这样介绍下去得介绍到什么时候……
t_en: I'm Evanescia, special observer for Phantasmoon Games, professional digital pet caretaker, eternally young and radiant high schooler... Ugh, but at this rate, how long is this introduction gonna take...?

t_ch: 不如…你来提问，我来回答！
t_en: How about this... you ask the questions, I'll give the answers!

t_ch: 球棒浣熊，找我有什么事吗？
t_en: Baseball Raccoon, what can I do for you?

t_ch: 漫画看完了，游戏也打腻了，没要紧事儿的话，我们来聊聊天怎么样~
t_en: Done with my comics, bored of my games... so hey, if you're not busy, wanna chat for a bit~?

t_ch: 什么都能问吗？
t_en: I can ask anything?

t_ch: 我肯定满足你的好奇心，问吧！
t_en: I'll totally satisfy your curiosity, so fire away!

t_ch: 关于《苍天航路绒绒号》……
t_en: About Fluffy Across the Blue...

t_ch: 看漫画嘛，除了欣赏剧情之外，人际关系也是不得不品的一环。
t_en: When it comes to reading comics, besides enjoying the plot, the relationships between characters are definitely something you gotta savor.

t_ch: 我一定要说，不懂列车长和杨叔的有难了。
t_en: I gotta say, anyone who doesn't get the conductor and Mr. Yang is in for a rough ride.

t_ch: 它，自银河中飘零而来；它，坚持开拓未知星海。美好的故事就此开始——去往未来的归途，也是彼此的救赎……
t_en: Adrift from the cosmos, it arrived... Committed to Trailblazing the uncharted sea of stars. And so begins a wondrous story. A journey toward the hereafter, and a path of mutual redemption...

t_ch: 笔给你，你来画！
t_en: Here's the pen, you draw it!

t_ch: 我？画画？算啦，漫画的事还是交给漫画家来做。不过，我会想办法兜售我的好点子哒！
t_en: Me? Draw? Nah, leave the comic stuff to the pros. But hey, I'll totally pitch my brilliant ideas!

t_ch: 回头转述给杨叔。
t_en: I'll pass it along to Mr. Yang later.

t_ch: 啊…我还存了几篇精彩的小说…我这就转给你，一起带给杨叔吧！
t_en: Oh... I've got a few great novels saved up too... I'll send them to you right now, and you can take them to Mr. Yang as well!

t_ch: 好想快点看到绒绒号里关于二相乐园的新篇章呀…名字就叫《疯狂幻月•神位争夺篇！》怎么样？一听就能火。
t_en: I can't wait to see the new chapter about Planarcadia on The Fluffy... How about calling it Crazy Phantasmoon: Battle for Godhood!? That title's got huge hit written all over it.

t_ch: 关于这一届幻月游戏……
t_en: About this year's Phantasmoon Games...

t_ch: 上一次这么热闹的游戏…让我想想，还是上上次。
t_en: The last time a game was this wild... let me think, it was the time before last.

t_ch: 月亮十五年满盈了两回，还不知道这次会有什么更欢愉、更有趣、更稀奇的事情发生呢。
t_en: The moon's waxed full twice over these fifteen years, and who knows what even more joyful, more amusing, more extraordinary things might happen this time around.

t_ch: 说不定，阿哈已经酝酿好了祂的银河级笑话，准备让所有人目瞪口呆。
t_en: Maybe Aha has already cooked up THEIR cosmic-level punchline, ready to blow everyone's minds.

t_ch: 唉，但一想到会闹出来的乱子…头疼。
t_en: Ugh, but just thinking about the chaos that'll ensue... gives me a headache.

t_ch: 你觉得谁会赢？
t_en: Who do you think will win?

t_ch: 凑近点，偷偷告诉你——
t_en: Lean in close, I'll let you in on a secret—

t_ch: 「我也不知道。」
t_en: I don't know either.

t_ch: 不过，你大可以自信点嘛，你可是「主角」呀。
t_en: But hey, you should have more confidence in yourself. You're the 'protagonist' after all.

t_ch: 有我在，放心。
t_en: I've got this, don't worry.

t_ch: 嗯，期待你的表现。我也会好好盯着的。
t_en: Mm, looking forward to your performance. I'll be keeping a close eye on things too.

t_ch: 你会怎么做？
t_en: What would you do?

t_ch: 二相乐园怪事多多，我早就习惯了，随机应变吧。
t_en: Planarcadia's full of weird stuff. I'm used to it by now. Gotta roll with the punches.

t_ch: 平时的爱好……
t_en: My hobbies...

t_ch: 看，我腰上挂着的电子宠物蛋。哼哼…这里面可装着我养的宠物军团。
t_en: See this digital pet gadget on my belt? Heh heh... It's holding my entire pet legion.

t_ch: 当时在网上疯狂找攻略，带着它们去逛公园，交朋友，做甜食喂给它们吃…好怀念刚刚接触这个游戏的时光。
t_en: I was scouring the net for walkthroughs and guides like crazy back then. Taking them to the park, making friends, baking sweets to feed them... Man, I miss those early days when I first got into this game.

t_ch: 不过二相乐园的潮流总是一阵一阵的，这次我发现二维市新建了好几座大楼，随便一逛就吃谷吃到爽…人真的会反复爱上列车长的棉花娃娃呢！
t_en: But Planarcadia's trends come and go in waves. This time I noticed several new buildings popped up in Duomension City. Just a quick stroll, and I was drowning in merch... I swear, I fall in love with the Conductor's cotton dolls over and over again!

t_ch: 一听就是老二次元了。
t_en: Ah, a true 2D fan, I see.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，你也可以安利我喜欢的作品呀！
t_en: Hehe, you can totally recommend stuff you like to me too!

t_ch: 我也很心动！
t_en: I'm tempted, too!

t_ch: 嗯，下次一起去吧！
t_en: Yeah, let's go together next time!

t_ch: 话说回来，真羡慕你可以搓揉帕姆，有机会我也好想试试，一定很幸福~
t_en: By the way, I'm so jealous you get to squish Pom-Pom! If I ever get the chance, I'd love to try it too. It must feel amazing~

t_ch: 没什么要问的了。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 二相乐园一天一个样，我得趁这几天去追赶一下新潮流了，回见~
t_en: Planarcadia changes every single day, so I gotta catch up with the latest trends while I can. See ya~

t_ch: 下次再聊！
t_en: Talk later!

t_ch: 二相乐园一天一个样，我得趁这几天去追赶一下新潮流了，回见~
t_en: Planarcadia changes every single day, so I gotta catch up with the latest trends while I can. See ya~

t_ch: 星穹列车的客人，你好。
t_en: Greetings, guests from the Astral Express.

t_ch: 请问，你有看见一只横冲直撞的像素轻脚吗？大概有两个我这么高。
t_en: Excuse me, have you seen a pixel lightpaw running amok? About twice my height.

t_ch: 没看见。
t_en: Didn't see it.

t_ch: 准确地说，那是只由上百只尘灵拼凑而成的大猫。
t_en: To be precise, it was a giant cat pieced together from hundreds of wispae.

t_ch: 受害者称，「它」行动敏捷，热衷于追逐路边的犬类，一旦试图凑近抓捕，尘灵们便会解体，四散躲藏到各个隐蔽的角落。异常防御部负责巡逻此地的犬灵，也成了被逗弄的对象。
t_en: Victims report that "it" moves with surprising agility and has a particular fondness for chasing street dogs. Whenever anyone tries to get close enough to catch them, the wispae disassemble and scatter into every nook and cranny they can find. Even the woofae patrolling the area for the Department of Aberration Defense have become targets of their mischief.

t_ch: 心疼治安犬们一秒。
t_en: Poor guard dogs. They have my sympathy. For a second.

t_ch: 尘灵这么顽皮吗？
t_en: Are wispae always this mischievous?

t_ch: 尘灵本身是微小的愿望，如果一大堆「猫猫追狗」的愿望聚集，就会变成这样。
t_en: Wispae are tiny wishes to begin with, so when a whole bunch of cat-chases-dog wishes pile up together, this is what you get.

t_ch: 游戏期间，欢愉之月播撒至人间的愿力增幅，此类幻造种捣乱事件层出不穷，你们也多加小心。
t_en: During the event, the Moon of Elation amplifies the Wishpower it spreads to the mortal realm, and these disturbances caused by imagenae occur incessantly, so you'd better watch yourselves out there.

t_ch: 抱歉，光顾着说工作的事了。找我有什么事吗？
t_en: Sorry, I got carried away talking about work. What did you need?

t_ch: 噢，是你呀。
t_en: Oh, it's you.

t_ch: 近日广场区域由我负责巡逻，有什么异常情况需要上报吗？
t_en: I'm on patrol duty around the plaza these days. Anything unusual to report?

t_ch: 想和你聊聊。
t_en: I want to have a chat with you.

t_ch: 想聊点什么？不过我正在执勤，我们尽量长话短说。
t_en: What's up? I'm on duty right now, so let's keep it quick.

t_ch: 你怎么看待幻月游戏？
t_en: What do you think of the Phantasmoon Games?

t_ch: 还记得游戏规则之一吗？「幻月游戏期间，谒者的所作所为，官方无权插手」。
t_en: Remember one of the game rules? "Officials have no right to interfere with a Supplicant's actions during the Phantasmoon Games."

t_ch: 这场以献礼星神为名的狂欢，表演者与观众都能尽兴，只有社会法规与道德约束会被弃置一旁。
t_en: In this carnival held in the name of honoring the Aeon, both performers and spectators can revel to their heart's content. Societal laws and moral restraints get tossed aside.

t_ch: 在此期间，治安事件明显增多：幻造种的异常创生、发狂；谒者、观众，甚至路人的意外伤亡；上一届游戏还发生了极其可怕的事……
t_en: During this period, public safety incidents spike dramatically: imagenae creating aberrations and turning frenzied, Supplicants, spectators and even passersby dying in accidents. And in the last round of games, something absolutely horrific went down...

t_ch: 具体细节就不说了。但无论局面如何失控，游戏仍会照常进行，这是这片欢愉之土唯一守常不变的规则。
t_en: I'll spare you the details. But no matter how uncontrollable it gets, the game goes on. That's the one constant rule in this land of Elation.

t_ch: 所以，一定还会有各种难以预料的情况发生。身为异常防御部的一员，我能做的只有尽己所能，保护好大家。
t_en: So there are bound to be all kinds of unpredictable situations ahead. As a member of the Department of Aberration Defense, all I can do is give it my all to keep everyone safe.

t_ch: 你的同事都是幻造种吗？
t_en: Are all your coworkers imagenae?

t_ch: 只是我们更擅长发掘幻造种的独特优势，辅助刑侦办案。所以除了犬灵，你能经常看到机铠、影鳄和其他幻造种治安官们，他们都是异常防御部的得力干将。
t_en: We're simply better at tapping into the unique advantages as an imagenae when it comes to supporting criminal investigations. So aside from woofae, you'll regularly see mechatrons, shadowcrocs, and other imagenae officers. They're all invaluable assets to the Department of Aberration Defense.

t_ch: 对了，你知道幻造种治安官们特殊的办案手法吗？
t_en: By the way, do you know about the imagenae security officers' special investigative methods?

t_ch: 是什么？
t_en: What is it?

t_ch: 这是可以透露的吗？
t_en: Is that something you can reveal?

t_ch: 没事，这不算什么机密。
t_en: It's fine, this isn't exactly classified info.

t_ch: 他们有个十分高效的特性，能在二维与三维形态间自如切换，自由出入二维空间——
t_en: They have this incredibly efficient trait that allows them to switch freely between two-dimensional and three-dimensional forms, so they can slip in and out of the 2D space at will.

t_ch: 我们科拿得出手的经典案件有许多——比如曾经有位轻脚治安官，潜入地下团伙「黑猫凶猛」，假装派系徽标十年，最终搜获了足量关键证据，将他绳之以法。
t_en: Our department has plenty of classic cases to boast about. Like the time a lightpaw security officer went undercover in the underground syndicate "Black Cat Cutthroats," posed as a faction member for ten years, and eventually gathered enough critical evidence to bring them to justice.

t_ch: 太有招了。
t_en: That's genius.

t_ch: 太能忍了。
t_en: That's some serious patience right there.

t_ch: 但如今利用这一特性，幻造种的违法犯罪活动也并不罕见，你们可以多加注意，谨慎一些总不会错。
t_en: But nowadays, criminal activities by imagenae exploiting this trait aren't exactly rare, so keep your eyes peeled. Better safe than sorry.

t_ch: 二相乐园有推荐的打卡地吗？
t_en: Any recommended must-see spots in Planarcadia?

t_ch: 鸽川区这种知名的打卡圣地你应该去过了？那里光彩陆离，拍照很好看。嗯…我想想…我最了解的还属美食店铺，你有兴趣吗？
t_en: You've probably already been to famous hotspots like Dovebrook District, right? The place is incredibly radiant and scenic, perfect for photos. Hmm... let me think... I know culinary shops the best. Interested?

t_ch: 想吃好吃的。
t_en: I want something yummy.

t_ch: 我推荐生生食食融合餐厅。开店几十年来从没下过二相乐园必吃榜第一名。
t_en: I recommend the Raw 'n' Roasted Fusion Diner. It's been at the top of Planarcadia's Must-Eat List for decades straight.

t_ch: 工作结束后，我都会去点一碗招牌辣炒三文鱼，配上海原市特产的尖椒鸭头…鲜美极了。
t_en: After work, I always grab a bowl of their signature spicy stir-fried salmon, paired with Seafeld City's specialty spicy duck heads... absolutely divine.

t_ch: 据说店内原材料都是店长亲自去往各地，调研品鉴的选择，不仅幻造种们吃完大呼「愿力」满满，我也是爱极了他们的三分熟拆骨肉排。
t_en: Word is the owner personally travels far and wide to source and sample the finest ingredients. Not only do the imagenae rave about how it fills them with "Wishpower," but I'm absolutely crazy about their medium-rare boneless steak.

t_ch: 想去能出片的。
t_en: I wanna go somewhere photogenic.

t_ch: 电视塔顶层的旋转餐厅，热门的庆生约会圣地。我个人觉得最好吃的是冻皮鳌虾，其他餐品中规中矩，但餐厅胜在能近距离观赏窗外的欢愉之月。
t_en: The rotating restaurant at the top of the TV tower is a popular spot for birthday celebrations and dates. Personally, I think the best dish is the frozen-skin lobster. The rest of the menu is pretty standard, but the restaurant's real draw is the close-up view of the Moon of Elation through the windows.

t_ch: 幸运的话，还能和外头的雷虬打招呼，他兴致高涨时总爱来「关心」一下人类。不过也有客人嫌他吵闹，那家伙实在太爱笑了。
t_en: If you're lucky, you might even get to say hi to the thundercoil outside. He loves to drop by and "check in" on us humans when he's in high spirits. Though some guests complain he's too noisy. That guy just laughs way too much.

t_ch: 改天我抽空做一份二相乐园的随吃随逛路线攻略给你吧。美食探店这一块，我确实很有自信。
t_en: I'll whip up a guide for you some other time, an eat-as-you-explore route through Planarcadia. When it comes to scoping out the finest food, I'm honestly pretty confident in my skills.

t_ch: 你总是这么忙碌吗？
t_en: Are you always this busy?

t_ch: 最近我能自由支配的时间确实不多…多谢你的提醒，我的确要好好安排下下班后的时刻了，让自己放松一下，劳逸结合。
t_en: Truth is, I haven't had much free time lately... Thanks for the reminder. I really should start planning my after-work hours better. You know, unwind a little and find that work-life balance.

t_ch: 幻月游戏期间，实在难得拥有一个完整的休息日…真怀念可以宅在家里浇浇花，看看美食吃播，就度过一天的清闲日子。
t_en: During the Phantasmoon Games, it's so rare to get a full day off... I really miss those days when I could just laze at home, water my plants, watch some food vlogs, and call it a day.

t_ch: 有推荐的吃播博主吗？
t_en: Got any food influencers to recommend?

t_ch: 说了你别意外——我会推荐火花。
t_en: You might be surprised, but I'd recommend Sparxie.

t_ch: 客观评价，她的吃播节目很优秀。虽然个子小小的，但吃什么都很香，看她的节目能让人感受到纯粹的快乐，也让人很有食欲。
t_en: Objectively speaking, her eating streams are excellent. She may be tiny, but she eats everything with such gusto. Watching her brings pure happiness and really works up your appetite.

t_ch: 这么说来，过去我也算半个火花的粉丝。鉴于她近来给异常防御部添的麻烦，她的吃播我应该是不会…不会天天看了。
t_en: Come to think of it, I used to be somewhat of a Sparxie fan myself. Given all the trouble she's caused the Department of Aberration Defense lately, I definitely won't be... won't be watching her eating streams every day anymore.

t_ch: 有养花的秘诀吗？
t_en: Got any tips for nurturing plants?

t_ch: 很简单，正常浇水与晒太阳，看到土壤干透了，就再用水和光喂饱它们就行。但晒多了吃撑了是很常见的事，所以需要时刻关注。
t_en: Easy peasy! Just water and sunlight as usual. When you see the soil's bone dry, give it another good drink and some rays. But it's common to overindulge them from too much sunlight, so you have to keep an eye on them.

t_ch: 只要你一直护着，看着，就会见到鲜花开得最盛的模样，那种不管不顾向外迸发的生命力，每一片花瓣都在用力舒展，像要把全部的能量都献给这个世界。
t_en: As long as you keep protecting and watching over it, you'll witness the flowers bloom as brilliantly as ever. That kind of raw, unstoppable vitality erupting outward, every petal stretching with all its might, like it's offering every ounce of energy into this world.

t_ch: 每次看到，都觉得自己的心也跟着打开了。
t_en: Every time I see that, I feel my heart blooming too.

t_ch: 不打扰你工作了。
t_en: I'll leave you to your work.

t_ch: 哪里的话。若有异常情况，随时联系我。
t_en: Don't mention it. If anything weird comes up, hit me up anytime.

t_ch: 再忙也要记得吃饭呀！
t_en: No matter how busy you are, don't forget to eat!

t_ch: 谢谢关心。等下次休息，我带各位去探店。
t_en: Thanks for your concern. Next time I get a break, I'll take you all to explore some shops.

t_ch: 真是少见，事务所居然来生意了。咦，是你啊……
t_en: Well, well, a customer here at the agency. That's a rare sight. Oh, it's you...

t_ch: 重新自我介绍下吧，在下不死途。如你所见，我是一名接取各类委托的侦探。
t_en: Let me reintroduce myself. My name's Ashveil. As you can see, I'm a detective who takes on all kinds of requests.

t_ch: 星穹列车的大人物专程前来拜访…难不成是有什么高价委托？
t_en: A bigwig from the Astral Express came all this way to visit... Don't tell me you've got some high-paying assignment for me?

t_ch: 只是碰巧逛到这里。
t_en: Just happened to pass by.

t_ch: 哈哈，没事就是最好的事——总之，欢迎你光顾「不死神探事务所」。不管是否带着委托来，至少…嗯，你的心情看上去不错。
t_en: Haha, no news is good news. Anyway, welcome to the Ashen Detective Agency. Whether you've got an assignment for me or not, at least... well, you seem to be in good spirits.

t_ch: 委托…你和我聊聊如何？
t_en: Assignments... How about you chat with me for a bit?

t_ch: 这种情况按句给钱吗？
t_en: Do I get paid per sentence for this kind of gig?

t_ch: 开玩笑的，咨询也是侦探工作的一环。
t_en: Just kidding, consulting's part of a detective's job too.

t_ch: 什么时候能接到足够多的委托呢…再这样下去，这个月的冰箱电费都快拿出不来了。
t_en: When are we gonna get enough cases... At this rate, I won't even be able to cover this month's electric bill for the fridge.

t_ch: 想找你问些事儿。
t_en: Wanted to ask you about something.

t_ch: 有什么想了解的，随便问吧。
t_en: Got any questions? Fire away.

t_ch: 你就住在事务所吗？
t_en: You live in the office?

t_ch: 没错。不然怎么能确保顾客顺利找到这间二楼报社里的杂物室？更何况，来这儿的路上还可能有刁滑的狸猫敲诈过路钱。
t_en: Yup. Otherwise, how would customers find this storage room tucked away on the second floor of a newspaper office? What's more, they might run into cunning furbos looking to swindle them along the way.

t_ch: 我要是不守在这，他们就算到了，看到摞地的纸盒、倒下的柜子和冰箱…恐怕也会以为这是杂志社萌宠们住的小窝，而不是一个侦探事务所……
t_en: If I don't stay here and keep watch, when they show up and see cardboard boxes stacked all over the place, toppled cabinets and refrigerators... they'll probably think this is a nest for the magazine publisher's pets, not a detective agency...

t_ch: 选址的时候没考虑过吗？
t_en: Didn't you consider this before picking the location?

t_ch: 身为侦探，当然具备心思敏锐，虑无不周的素质。或许你以为我在反其道而行，但选在这里的原因只有一个……
t_en: As a detective, I naturally possess a sharp mind and meticulous consideration. You might think I'm doing the opposite, but there's only one reason I chose this place...

t_ch: 打算如何解决这个问题？
t_en: How do you plan to solve this problem?

t_ch: 解决？为什么要解决？找到问题和解决问题是两码事。
t_en: Solve it? Why would I solve it? Finding a problem and fixing a problem are two completely different things.

t_ch: 打个比方，侦探的工作或许包括查证与追捕，但绝不包括直接干掉凶手——那是另外的价钱。不换地址的原因很简单……
t_en: For example, a detective's job might include investigating and tracking down suspects, but it definitely doesn't include directly taking out the culprit. That's a different price tag. The reason for not changing addresses is simple...

t_ch: 没钱，{F#小姐}{M#兄弟}——这是一道单选题，就这么简单。
t_en: I'm broke, {F#miss}{M#buddy}. This is a single-choice question. Elementary.

t_ch: 除去冰箱电费，衣食花销和新鲜香蕉支出…我只能租得起这个无人问津的报社里一个犄角旮旯的空间了。
t_en: After covering the refrigerator's electric bill, food and clothing expenses, and the cost of some fresh banana... all I could afford was this cramped space in a corner of a forgotten newspaper firm.

t_ch: 旁边这位是……
t_en: And this person beside you is...

t_ch: 老白是我的助手。
t_en: Mister N is my assistant.

t_ch: 侦探的声音一如往常的低沉，却铿锵有力。而一份异样的情感在他心中油然而生——他不能让旁人将并肩同行的伙伴误认为是宠物了。
t_en: The detective's voice was as deep as ever, yet carried a firm resolve. A peculiar emotion welled up within him, he couldn't let others mistake his traveling companion for a pet.

t_ch: 嗯，如你所见，它是我的「旁白」。
t_en: Yeah, as you can see, it's my "Narrator."

t_ch: 为了证明同伴的重要性，也为了保护助手的自尊心，当然，也可能是台词不过脑子——侦探选择了一种暧昧的说辞。
t_en: It's all to prove the importance of his companion, and to protect his assistant's self-esteem. And of course, possibly because his mouth moves faster than his brain, the detective opted for an ambiguous answer.

t_ch: 老白，你今天心情很不错啊。
t_en: Mister N, you're in a pretty good mood today.

t_ch: 你们认识很久了吗？
t_en: Have you two known each other long?

t_ch: 你们关系真好。
t_en: You two seem to get along very well.

t_ch: 嗯。不得不说，时间已经让咱俩无比默契了，他很懂我。
t_en: Mm. I gotta say, time's made us totally in sync. He really gets me.

t_ch: 「很懂」——指我能叫醒早晨睡死过去的他。之前「诗人」和「乐手」来二相乐园时，就从没叫起过他——现在他们两位，已经在其他星系养老了。
t_en: "Really gets me" means I'm the one who can actually wake him up in the morning when he's dead to the world. Back when the "Poet" and the "Musician" visited Planarcadia, they never managed to get him out of bed. Now, those two are retired and living it up in some other star system.

t_ch: 不死途先生总说羡慕我的口才，其实我也是师从他们两位。还有……
t_en: Mr. Ashveil always says he envies my way with words, but truth is, I learned from both of them. And also...

t_ch: 喂喂老白，你今天话密了。
t_en: Hey, hey, Mister N, you're being awfully chatty today.

t_ch: 侦探眼眸低垂，心中闪过一丝警惕…好吧抱歉，怪我今天心情太好了。
t_en: The detective's eyes narrowed, a flash of wariness flickering through his mind... Okay fine, sorry. My bad for being in such a good mood today.

t_ch: 总之，你大可相信我和老白的职业素养。
t_en: Anyway, you can totally trust me and Mister N's professionalism.

t_ch: 师傅，你是做什么的？
t_en: What do you do for work, sir?

t_ch: 看你问的…在你眼中，侦探难道就是个伪装身份？
t_en: For you to ask that... Do you think being a detective is just some kind of cover?

t_ch: 我知道你在想什么：在成为侦探之前，我应该是位诞生自「极寒之地」的圣贤，或者是看似外表普通实则心思缜密的杀人狂，再差也得是个与魔鬼签订契约的普通人…噢，漏了一个，家族被灭后韬光养晦的复仇王子……
t_en: I know what you're thinking: before becoming a detective, I must've been a Sage born in the "frozen land," or a seemingly normal person who's actually a meticulous psychotic killer, or at the very least, some average Joe who signed a contract with the devil... Oh, almost forgot one, a prince of revenge lying low after his family had been wiped out...

t_ch: 他通通不是。
t_en: He's none of those.

t_ch: 但我确实涉猎很广，诸如银行的押钞员，幻月游戏某分会场保镖，写字楼的维修工，也接过养老院家长们的外包儿女委托…这些事儿我统统干过。
t_en: But I've got a pretty diverse resume: cash-in-transit officer for banks, bouncer at a Phantasmoon Games venue, maintenance guy for commercial buildings, I've even taken on assignments from nursing home residents to care for them like their children... I've done it all.

t_ch: 淡淡的忧伤缓慢爬上男人的脸庞，那是面对岁月刀光的窘迫，是对逝去青春的回望——以往他能一天干五份活，如今他只能完成「三步将自己塞进冰箱」。
t_en: A faint melancholy slowly creeps across the man's face. The awkwardness of facing time's relentless blade, a glance back at his youth long gone. He used to juggle five jobs a day. Now, he can barely bring himself to move "three steps to stuff himself into a refrigerator."

t_ch: 你的生意看着有点寡淡啊……
t_en: Business is looking pretty slow, huh...

t_ch: 这都被你发现了，很敏锐啊。
t_en: You caught that? Sharp eye.

t_ch: 我们也尝试过打广告，报刊上的大版位费用负担不起，就退而求其次登在最便宜的「每日幽默」栏位里，当然，结果是被人当成笑话……
t_en: We tried advertising too, but full-page spreads in the papers were way out of our budget, so we settled for the cheapest option: the "Daily Humor" section. And of course... people thought it was a joke...

t_ch: 侦探心下偷喜，为了追赶潮流想了好几天才想出来的一语双关的笑话，终于被他逮着机会讲了。
t_en: The detective was secretly thrilled. After racking his brain for days trying to come up with a trendy pun, he'd finally gotten the chance to use it.

t_ch: 也盯上过外面那家杂志社，老白趁着它们印刷新刊时偷偷将封底的照片换成了事务所的。本来是个很成功的计划……
t_en: He also had his eye on that magazine publisher outside. Mister N snuck in while they were printing a new issue and swapped out the back cover photo with one of the agency's. It was supposed to be a masterstroke...

t_ch: 结果那群狸猫一个比一个精明，为了省钱脸都不…封面封底都不要了，也不装订，就直接一张张地发…这跟发传单有什么区别？
t_en: Turns out those furbos are sharper than you'd think. To save some money, they didn't even care about losing face... They ditched the front and back covers, skipped the binding, and just handed out loose pages... How's that any different from handing out flyers?

t_ch: 你好，我是杂志社新老板。
t_en: Hey there, I'm the magazine publisher's new boss.

t_ch: 老板你好。
t_en: Hey there, boss.

t_ch: 老板你好。
t_en: Hey there, boss.

t_ch: 情报服务，寻人跟踪，推理课程——「不死神探事务所」潜力股看一看。如果以上都不符合，您要他做什么都可以。
t_en: Intelligence services, searching for missing persons, deduction courses: check out the rising star, "Ashen Detective Agency." If none of the above fit your needs, he'll do whatever you want.

t_ch: 对。啊？不对。
t_en: Yeah. Wait, no.

t_ch: 也许你可以试试网络直播。
t_en: Maybe you could give live streaming a shot.

t_ch: 试过。可惜，事务所的卖相并不吸人眼球，我的卖相虽然勉强可用，但网络上奇人异事多了去了，对比之下我的直播就太平庸了。虽然老白安慰我说，是没找到合适的赛道。
t_en: Tried it. Unfortunately, the agency's aesthetics aren't exactly eye-catching, and while my own looks are barely passable, the internet's crawling with all sorts of wild characters and bizarre content. Compared to them, my livestreams were just too bland. Though Mister N tried to comfort me, saying I just hadn't found the right niche yet.

t_ch: 和猴子比挤眉弄眼，挑战睡醒后单眼皮变九眼皮，还有沉浸式睡眠…不死途先生，很遗憾地告诉你，现在的世界是以内容为王。
t_en: Making faces at monkeys, all-nighter challenges, immersive sleep experiences... Mr. Ashveil, I regret to inform you that in today's world, content is king.

t_ch: 好吧。我确实该迈开腿脚追一追年轻人的步伐了。
t_en: Alright. I really should get off my butt and keep up with the youngsters.

t_ch: 早起的老狼有肉吃，机会就在眼前。不死途先生，我们该努努力了，先从早睡早起做起。
t_en: The early wolf gets the meat, and opportunity's knocking on your door right now. Mr. Ashveil, we gotta step up our game. Start with hitting the hay early and rising with the sun.

t_ch: 没什么要问的了。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 好的。有任何委托随时找我，价格都好说。
t_en: Sure thing. If you ever need any assignments done, just hit me up! I'm flexible on the price.

t_ch: 现在知道你深藏不露了……
t_en: Now I see you're not one to show your hand easily...

t_ch: …啊对，就是这个反应。
t_en: Oh, yes... that's the reaction.

t_ch: 不少委托人在亲眼见识过我的破案技巧后，都会发出类似的赞叹，你的叹服，我也接下了。
t_en: Plenty of clients have sung similar praises after witnessing my detective skills in action. I'll gladly accept your admiration as well.

t_ch: 你好，巡海游侠领猎人。
t_en: Hello, leader of the Galaxy Rangers.

t_ch: 嘘，游侠不问出处。不过你要是坚持这么喊，之后商议的委托可得加钱了。毕竟比起「不死神探」，这个头衔很难给您打折啊——它可能会引来麻烦的回头客。
t_en: Shh, a Ranger's past is their own. Keep shouting that name, though, and my rates are going up. After all, compared to "Ashen Detective," this title doesn't exactly lend itself to discounts... It might attract certain troublesome repeat customers.

t_ch: 实际上我们心知肚明，那个头衔就像街边被风吹日晒的过期悬赏单，现在揭下来，也兑不了几个信用点。
t_en: Truth is, we all know deep down that title's like a sun-bleached bounty poster peeling off a wall. Even if you cashed it in now, you wouldn't get more than a handful of credits.

t_ch: 总之，这把老骨头也经不起大的折腾了，现在我更习惯等麻烦自己找上门，至少能省下几双鞋的钱。
t_en: Anyway, these old bones can't take much of a beating anymore. I prefer waiting for trouble to come knocking at my door these days. That way, at least it saves me a few pairs of shoes.

t_ch: 所以在这儿我只是个侦探，还是叫我「不死途」吧，这名字更吉利，听着就长寿。
t_en: Here, I'm merely a detective, nothing more. Just call me Ashveil. This name feels like it'd bring good luck.

t_ch: 拉曼查，我叫你你敢答应吗？
t_en: La Mancha, if I call your name, dare you respond?

t_ch: 三秒过去了，话掉地上了。侦探揉了揉眉心，看似是突发了某种古老的偏头痛，实则是在懊恼自己没能立刻想出一句搪塞对方的俏皮话。
t_en: Three seconds have passed. The ball is in his court. The detective rubs the spot between his eyebrows, seemingly struck by some ancient migraine, but really just kicking himself for not having a snappy comeback ready to fire back.

t_ch: 他意识到，除了《推理入门》，自己还需要进修一本《接话的艺术》。
t_en: He realizes that apart from Detective Deduction 101, he also needs to study up on The Fine Art of Small Talk.

t_ch: 诶。一个名字而已，又不会掉块肉。
t_en: Well, it's just a name. It won't bite.

t_ch: 只不过隔墙有耳，叫这个名字可能引发的代价，你未必想付，我也未必想收。还是简单点好。
t_en: You never know who's listening. The price that comes with using that name is one you might not want to pay, and one I might not want to collect. So why don't we just keep things simple?

t_ch: 所以，在这儿我只是个侦探，还是叫我「不死途」吧，这名字更吉利，听着就长寿。
t_en: Here, I'm merely a detective, nothing more. Just call me Ashveil. This name feels like it'd bring good luck.

t_ch: 下次再聊！
t_en: Talk later!

t_ch: 好的。有任何委托随时找我，价格都好说。
t_en: Sure thing. If you ever need any assignments done, just hit me up! I'm flexible on the price.

t_ch: 就那欢愉之月眨眼的频率来看，今时此地，你定会出现在我的面前。
t_en: Judging by the frequency of the Moon of Elation's twinkling, I knew you'd show up here and now, right in front of me.

t_ch: 说说吧，想找我聊些什么？卜卦、解卦、还是八卦？
t_en: So, what brings you here? Fortune-telling, fortune reading, or just plain old gossip?

t_ch: 你怎么在这儿？
t_en: Why are you here?

t_ch: 花火，又是你。
t_en: Sparkle, it's you again.

t_ch: 是我本人噢。
t_en: I'm the real deal.

t_ch: 嗯…欢歌笑语也算是白噪音的一种，正适合在此磨练心性。
t_en: Hmm... Laughter and chatter can be considered a form of white noise, perfect for tempering one's mental fortitude here.

t_ch: 放心，愚者们可不会闲着关注我一个偶然的「外来之人」。
t_en: Relax, the Fools aren't gonna waste their time keeping tabs on some random "outsider" like me.

t_ch: 好了，我瞧你今日心境开阔，三分气定神闲，三分走马观花，三分想找乐子，一分闲来无事。我也正巧从一些政务琐事里偷得一刻闲散。走前咱们聊会儿天罢？
t_en: Well well, I can see you're in quite a good mood today: three parts zen, three parts leisure, three parts hunting for funsies, and one part just killing time. As it happens, I've also managed to catch a brief break from my pile of tedious administrative work. How about we chat for a bit before you go?

t_ch: 想知道什么尽管问，爻老板我呢，知无不言。
t_en: Ask me anything you wanna know. I'll tell you everything I know.

t_ch: 又被你撞到我在这儿偷闲了？
t_en: Caught me slacking off here again?

t_ch: 说说吧，想聊些什么？卜卦、解卦、八卦——爻老板我呢，知无不言。
t_en: So, what brings you here? Fortune-telling, fortune reading, or juicy gossip? I'll tell you everything I know.

t_ch: 那我可要开始问了。
t_en: Then I'm going to fire away.

t_ch: 嚯，这么认真的表情，我倒开始好奇你会问出什么有趣的问题了。
t_en: Whoa, that's a serious look. I'm actually curious what kind of interesting questions you're gonna hit me with.

t_ch: 听说爻老板卜卦超准？
t_en: I heard your divinations are insanely accurate?

t_ch: 准与不准，往往在于卜者捕捉到的信息是否完全，我呢，不巧能「眼」观六路。但万千事物不断变换，生变之时即在寸阴之间……
t_en: Whether a divination hits or misses often depends on how complete the information the diviner picks up is. Me? I happen to have "eyes" that see in all directions. But the myriad forms of things are constantly shifting. Change can happen in the blink of an eye...

t_ch: 比如有一天，我算到星神会在某一刻欣赏地瞥你一眼，谁成想祂心情不好，白了你一眼。你看，一瞬之间，阴阳交错了不是？
t_en: For instance, I could predict that an Aeon will glance at you approvingly at a certain moment, but who'd have thought THEY'd be in a bad mood and give you the stink eye instead? See? In the blink of an eye, the variables flicker and intertwine to form a disaster, right?

t_ch: 所以呀，卦象涨落、吉凶命数，仅是一点前路的预示，可以当作警醒。但要我说，算得准不如行动快，遇事亲自上手，也省去了瞻前顾后的工夫。
t_en: So yeah, hexagrams of divinations shift, the predictions of luck and misfortune are simply faint hints of what lies ahead, good for keeping you on your toes. But if you ask me, being quick to act beats accurate predictions any day. If you encounter anything, get moving right away. Saves you all that second-guessing too.

t_ch: 我想算一卦，当个提醒。
t_en: I wanna get my fortune told, just for a reminder.

t_ch: 那回我瞧了你许久，却仅仅得出个「无从卜测」的结果。卜算讲究一个天时玄妙，今日换种方式如何？来，把手给我。
t_en: Last time I observed you for quite a while, but all I got was a reading that concluded you were "impossible to predict." Divination relies on cosmic timing and mystical alignment. How about we try a different approach today? Come, give me your hand.

t_ch: 果然成长势头正盛啊。赠你一言：「不留今，与时变，与俗化」。保持这股冲劲继续向前吧。
t_en: You're at the peak of your growth now. Here's some wisdom for you: "Don't cling to the present, change with the times, adapt to the world." Keep up this momentum and charge ahead.

t_ch: 受教了！
t_en: Got it!

t_ch: 记得先前为你卜卦，结果是「无从卜测」。
t_en: I remember when I tried to read your fortune before, the result was "impossible to predict."

t_ch: 没有既定结果，便预示着你的未来有无穷的可能。所以，想做什么的话，放手去做就好。
t_en: To have no fixed outcome means your future is brimming with infinite possibilities. So if there's something you want to do, just go for it.
